chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّهُ لَا طَلَاقَ قَبْلَ النِّكَاحِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 6 – There is no divorce before the copulation

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/106)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/106)
text: كَانَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِنَا يَقُولُونَ لَا عَتَاقَ وَ لَا طَلَاقَ إِلَّا بَعْدَ مَا يَمْلِكُ الرَّجُلُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Shuayb Bin Yaqoub, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The ones<sup>asws</sup> before us<sup>asws</sup> were saying: ‘There is neither emancipation nor a divorce except after what the man owns’.
en.sarwar: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Shu’ayb ibn Ya‘qub who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Those who were before us would say, ‘Setting free of a slave or divorce does not have any valid effect until a man has an ownership and marriage.’”
